articles.title: April set to hit dry spell after monthslong dry tilt
articles.author: Kevin Myatt

April showers bring May flowers. But April showers may be in short supply.

We are entering what is likely to be a prolonged bone-dry period in Virginia, following what has already been several months of below-normal precipitation that has left the majority of the commonwealth in moderate to severe drought on the latest U.S. Drought Monitor.

articles.title: Know the difference between heat exhaustion and heat stroke
articles.body:
As spring heats up and gives way to summer, it’s important to know the signs of heat exhaustion and heat stroke.

Heat usually peaks between noon-3 p.m. so it’s best to avoid going outdoors during these hours. If you must, try limiting yourself to short bursts. Take breaks and be sure you are well hydrated.

As you hydrate, avoid caffeinated or alcoholic beverages. Be sure to drink water throughout stopping periods, but never drink ice-cold water too fast when you’re overheated. If you are overh

They hold supermajorities in Montgomery, dominate statewide offices, and routinely win congressional seats by margins that would be unthinkable in most of the country. articles.author: Ed Oliver
articles.body:
Alabama’s economy runs on rail.

From the farms of the Tennessee Valley to the industries of Birmingham and the docks at the Port of Mobile, more than 3,000 miles of freight rail knit the state together. That network allows producers in places like Northeast Alabama and along Lake Guntersville to move goods efficiently to global markets, turning crops, timber and raw materials into economic growth.

That system works because it is competitive. And that’s exactly what’s at risk.

articles.title: Opinion | Closing Alabama’s primaries won’t strengthen the GOP; it will weaken accountability
articles.author: David Donaldson
articles.body:
As a native Alabamian from a long line of Republicans, I’m deeply troubled by House Bill 541 and the push to close Alabama’s primaries. I attended the Senate committee hearing on Tuesday and had the privilege to speak. I was struck by the gap between the arguments offered by proponents and what Alabama’s election data actually shows.

Advocates for HB541 insist the primary is merely a private party selection process. The “real election” where all voters get their say is in November, they argue.

articles.title: New Alabama Law Targets Birmingham’s Vacant Properties with Registry, Fees

The legislation creates a vacant property registry aimed at holding property owners accountable and speeding up the process for addressing dilapidated buildings across the city.

Under the law, the registration fee is $250 with a 150% increase per year, capping at $1,000.

articles.title: Alabama lawmakers pass farm utility tax relief bill
articles.author: Staff
articles.body:
The Alabama Legislature on Thursday gave final passage to Senate Bill 159 by Senator Jack Williams, R-Wilmer, and Representative Danny Crawford, R-Athens.

Senate Bill 159 exempts certain agricultural operations from Alabama’s utility gross receipts and utility service use taxes. The bill removes taxes on natural gas and electricity used in commercial aquaculture aeration systems, greenhouses, pivot irrigation systems and poultry houses. The exemption will take effect September 1, 2026.

articles.title: COLUMN: I’ve never been to Oklahoma, but I’ve been to Spain

articles.title: Valley Grande sets all-day celebration for youth sports opening ceremony
articles.author: Nichlos Cutrell
articles.body:
VALLEY GRANDE – The Valley Grande City Council announced Monday, April 6, that the city’s Parks and Recreation department will host its annual baseball and softball opening day ceremony on Saturday, April 18. The day-long event will be headlined by a ceremonial first pitch from AHSAA Hall of Famer Earl Griffin.

articles.title: Selma makes deep appearance in tennis sectionals
articles.author: Nichlos Cutrell
articles.body:
MONTGOMERY – The Selma High tennis program demonstrated significant grit at the AHSAA Class 4-5A, Section 3 tournament, April 9-10, led by a deep postseason run from standout America Salas.

Competing at O’Conner Tennis Center in Montgomery, the Saints faced a challenging field featuring powerhouses like Loveless Academic Magnet Program (LAMP) and Montgomery Catholic. articles.title: Troy Public Library hosts April 23 meet-and-greet with new director Stephenie Kelly
articles.author: Josh Boutwell
articles.body:
The Troy Public Library will be hosting a special meet-and-greet with new Library Director Stephenie Kelly on Thursday, April 23.

Kelly was recently named the new library director and comes to the Troy Public Library with more than a decade of experience as a librarian in the area with Troy City Schools.

articles.title: City of Troy Queens crowned
articles.author: Staff Report
articles.body:
Recently, the Miss Troy Pageant was held and the City of Troy Queens were crowned.

Julianna Knight was crowned Miss Troy, while Everly Lloyd McLendon was crowned Little Miss Troy. Both McLendon and Knight will represent the City of Troy at the National Peanut Festival Pageant in Dothan later this year. McLendon and Knight are also currently working on their community service initiatives.

articles.title: Blake Carnley’s family keeps his memory alive through giving
articles.author: Josh Boutwell
articles.body:
Since the passing of Blake Carnley, his family has worked to keep his memory alive through various charity and community events. This year will be no different as the family has partnered with Pike County DHR to donate to its adult services program.

articles.title: Troy earns conference series win over Texas State
articles.author: Josh Boutwell
articles.body:
The Troy Trojans earned a 3-2 Sun Belt Conference series win over the Texas State Bobcats this weekend, sweeping the final two games.

The series win moves Troy into a five-way tie for third place in the conference along with Southern Miss, South Alabama, ULM and Marshall.

articles.title: Statewide Fire Alert issued
articles.author: Staff Report
articles.body:
On Monday, Alabama State Forester Rick Oates issued a statewide Fire Alert effective immediately after worsening drought conditions across Alabama.

Over the past weekend, 50 wildfires burned more than 2,300 acres of forestland across Alabama, according to a statement from Oates. These fires included a 500-acre wildfire in Mobile County, a 412-acre wildfire in Close County, a 328-acre fire in Choctaw County, a 260-acre fire in Washington County and a 105-acre fire in Escambia County. articles.title: Beulah Cemetery hosting inaugural Guided Veterans Tour April 25
articles.author: Josh Boutwell
articles.body:
The Beulah Cemetery Committee will be hosting its inaugural Guided Veterans Tour at Historic Beulah Cemetery in Troy on April 25.

Last October, the Beulah Cemetery Committee hosted its first ever guided tour during Halloween.
